LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TED WIN WONG AND ROGER LOUIE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THEY ARE A REAL PARTY IN INTEREST, AS REQUIRED BY CCP 367. NOWHERE IN THE COMPLAINT DOES IT ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFFS ARE "MEMBERS" OF THE CORPORATION. (SEE CAL. CORP. CODE 5056(D), PROVIDING THAT A PERSON WHO HAS VOTING RIGHTS BY VIRTUE OF BEING A DIRECTOR IS NOT A MEMBER UNDER 5056(A).) WHERE A COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION IN SOMEONE, BUT NOT IN THE PLAINTIFF, A GENERAL DEMURRER FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION WILL BE SUSTAINED. (KLOPSTOCK V. SUPERIOR COURT (1941) 17 CAL.2D 13, 19.) IN THE ALTERNATIVE, IT IS UNCERTAIN HOW PLAINTIFFS (AND NOT THE CORPORATION) WERE INJURED BY THE ACTIONS OF THE TWO DEFENDANT BOARD MEMBERS. WHERE IT CANNOT BE ASCERTAINED FROM THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT HOW OR IN WHAT MANNER THE PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN DAMAGED, A SPECIAL DEMURRER FOR UNCERTAINTY IS PROPERLY SUSTAINED. (OPPENHEIMER V. GENERAL CABLE CORP. (1956) 143 CAL.APP.2D 293, 298-99.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TED WONG, ROGER LOUIE AND CHINESE CONSOLIDATED BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ASSUMING THAT THE SUCCESSIVE MOTION TO STRIKE WAS PROPER, CCP 446 ALLOWS FOR VERIFICATION BASED ON INFORMATION AND BELIEF. FURTHERMORE, "THE OBJECT OF A VERIFICATION IS TO ASSURE GOOD FAITH IN THE AVERMENTS OR STATEMENTS OF A PARTY." FRIO V. SUPERIOR CRT. (1988) 203 CAL.APP.3D 1480, 1498. GENERALLY, WHERE THERE IS NO ALLEGATION OF BAD FAITH, A CLAIM OF INADEQUATE VERIFICATION IS WITHOUT MERIT. SEE LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS V. EU, (1992) 7 CAL.APP.4TH 649, 656. "THE DEFECT [IMPROPER VERIFICATION] IS ONE OF FORM WHICH DOES NOT AFFECT THE SUBSTANCE." SEE SOLTANI-RASTEGAR V. SUP. CRT., (1989) 208 CAL.APP.3D 424, 428. HERE, THERE IS NO ALLEGATION OF BAD FAITH. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TED WONG & ROGER LOUIE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ('FAC') ADEQUATELY PLEADS THAT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BEING ASSERTED DERIVATIVELY BY MEMBERS OF THE CCBA ON BEHALF OF THE CCBA. (FAC, 94, 99.) TAKING EVERYTHING PLED IN THE FAC AS TRUE FOR PURPOSES OF DEMURRER, AS THE COURT MUST DO, THE BUSINESS JUDGMENT RULE DOES NOT BAR THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FAC ALLEGES THAT THE DEFENDANT DIRECTORS DID NOT ACT WITH GOOD FAITH, DID NOT ACT IN THE BEST INTERESTS OF CCBA, AND HAVE CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. (FAC, 39-40, 50, 52-53, 70, 81.) AT THE PLEADINGS STAGE, THIS IS SUFFICIENT. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE IS 'A JUSTICIABLE CONTROVERSY' THAT IS 'DEFINITE, CONCRETE, TOUCHING THE LEGAL RELATIONS OF PARTIES HAVING ADVERSE INTERESTS.' (LEPAGE V. CITY OF OAKLAND (1970) 13 CAL.APP.3D 689, 692.) THE FAC ALLEGES FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A DISPUTE BETWEEN MEMBERS OF A CORPORATION ON HOW THE BYLAWS SHOULD BE PROPERLY INTERPRETED AND APPLIED. (FAC, 103.) THE UNCERTAINTY ARGUMENT SUSTAINED AS TO THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT HAS BEEN CURED BY THE FAC BEING BROUGHT AS A DERIVATIVE ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TED WONG & ROGER LOUIE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DENIED AS TO THE OTHER REQUESTS TO STRIKE LANGUAGE REFERRING TO THE PLAINTIFFS AS INDIVIDUALS BECAUSE THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS BEING BROUGHT BY PLAINTIFFS IN THEIR INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY. DENIED AS TO THE REQUEST TO STRIKE ATTORNEY'S FEES LANGUAGE. IF THE CORPORATION OBTAINS A MONETARY RECOVERY FROM A SUCCESSFUL DERIVATIVE SUIT, THE PLAINTIFF SHAREHOLDER IS ENTITLED TO REIMBURSEMENT FOR REASONABLE LEGAL FEES AND LITIGATION COSTS INCURRED IN PROSECUTING THE ACTION. (CZIRAKI V. THUNDER CATS, INC. (2003) 111 CAL.APP.4TH 552, 557-58.) DENIED AS TO THE REQUEST TO STRIKE THE ACCOUNTING PORTION OF THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF. THE FAC ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT LOUIE HAD A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO THE CCBA AND THAT FUNDS MAY HAVE BEEN MISUSED IN CONNECTION WITH THE SCHOOL RENOVATION PROJECT. (FAC, 34, 53; SEE KRITZER V. MOTION For Order Appointing Referee
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CHRISTOPHER W. KATZENBACH, ESQ. of Katzenbach Law Offices, Tel No. 415-834-1778 appearing for Plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Nominal Defendant Chinese Consolidated Benevolent Association's ("CCBA") Motion for Order Appointing Referee is DENIED. The Taggares v. Superior Court, 62 Cal. App. 4th 94, 105 (1998) opinion discusses the high standard for appointing a discovery referee where the parties do not agree to it: "Unless both parties have agreed to a reference, the court should not make blanket orders directing all discovery motions to a discovery referee except in the unusual case where a majority of factors favoring reference are present. These include: (1) there are multiple issues to be resolved; (2) there are multiple motions to be heard simultaneously; (3) the present motion is only one in a continuum of many; (4) the number of documents to be reviewed (especially in issues based on assertions or privilege) make the inquiry inordinately time-consuming." CCBA has not made an adequate showing to justify a discovery referee under this standard. The discovery issues discussed in CCBA's motion concern one deposition -- the CCBA custodian of records. The only motion referenced is a motion to compel that deposition. It appears from Plaintiffs' opposition that there have also been some disagreements over scheduling of depositions. Based on this record, the Judge Pro Tem cannot conclude that a "majority of factors favoring reference" are present. Rather, these appear to be issues that the parties can work out on their own or, barring that, address through regular law and motion practice. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported (302/JPT)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ZESARA CHAN, ESQ., 415.421.6500, FOR PLAINTIFF CHARLES C. CHOW ET AL; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs TED WONG, ROGER LOUIE and CHINESE CONSOLIDATED BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Assuming that the successive motion to strike was proper, CCP 446 allows for verification based on information and belief. Furthermore, "the object of a verification is to assure good faith in the averments or statements of a party." Frio v. Superior Crt. (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1480, 1498. Generally, where there is no allegation of bad faith, a claim of inadequate verification is without merit. See League of Women Voters v. Eu, (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 649, 656. "The defect [improper verification] is one of form which does not affect the substance." See Soltani-Rastegar v. Sup. Crt., (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 424, 428. Here, there is no allegation of bad faith. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ZESARA CHAN, ESQ., 415.421.6500, FOR PLAINTIFFS CHARLES C. CHOW ET AL; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ted WONG & ROGER LOUIE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. As to the first and second causes of action, the First Amended Complaint ('FAC') adequately pleads that the causes of action are being asserted derivatively by members of the CCBA on behalf of the CCBA. (FAC, 94, 99.) Taking everything pled in the FAC as true for purposes of demurrer, as the Court must do, the Business Judgment Rule does not bar these causes of action. The FAC alleges that the defendant directors did not act with good faith, did not act in the best interests of CCBA, and have conflicts of interest. (FAC, 39-40, 50, 52-53, 70, 81.) At the pleadings stage, this is sufficient. As to the third cause of action, there is 'a justiciable controversy' that is 'definite, concrete, touching the legal relations of parties having adverse interests.' (LePage v. City of Oakland (1970) 13 Cal.App.3d 689, 692.) The FAC alleges facts that establish a dispute between members of a corporation on how the bylaws should be properly interpreted and applied. (FAC, 103.) The uncertainty argument sustained as to the original complaint has been cured by the FAC being brought as a derivative action. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ZESARA CHAN, ESQ., 415.421.6500, FOR PLAINTIFFS CHARLES C. CHOW ET AL; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs TED WONG & ROGER LOUIE's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to: (1) Page 3, 8, line 20, the phrase 'Plaintiffs, other CCBA Board members'; (2) Page 23, 95, line 12, the phrase 'Plaintiffs and'; (3) Page 23, 96, line 21, the phrase 'Plaintiffs and'; and (4) Page 24, 101, line 10, the phrase 'Plaintiffs and.' Denied as to the other requests to strike language referring to the Plaintiffs as individuals because the declaratory relief cause of action is being brought by Plaintiffs in their individual capacity. Denied as to the request to strike attorney's fees language. If the corporation obtains a monetary recovery from a successful derivative suit, the plaintiff shareholder is entitled to reimbursement for reasonable legal fees and litigation costs incurred in prosecuting the action. (Cziraki v. Thunder Cats, Inc. (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 552, 557-58.) Denied as to the request to strike the accounting portion of the prayer for relief. The FAC alleges that Defendant Louie had a fiduciary duty to the CCBA and that funds may have been misused in connection with the school renovation project. (FAC, 34, 53; see Kritzer v. Lancaster (1950) 96 Cal.App.2d 1, 6-7.) Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332

Defendants Ted Win Wong And Roger Louie'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ZESARA C. CHAN OF SHARTSIS FRIESE LLP, TEL. NO. 421-6500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JOHN K. KAO, TEL. NO. 362-9390 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Ted Win Wong And Roger Louie's Demurrer To Complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs have not established that they are a real party in interest, as required by CCP 367. Nowhere in the Complaint does it establish that Plaintiffs are "members" of the Corporation. (See Cal. Corp. Code 5056(d), providing that a person who has voting rights by virtue of being a director is not a member under 5056(a).) Where a complaint states a cause of action in someone, but not in the plaintiff, a general demurrer for failure to state a cause of action will be sustained. (Klopstock v. Superior Court (1941) 17 Cal.2d 13, 19.) In the alternative, it is uncertain how Plaintiffs (and not the corporation) were injured by the actions of the two defendant board members. Where it cannot be ascertained from the allegations of the complaint how or in what manner the plaintiff has been damaged, a special demurrer for uncertainty is properly sustained. (Oppenheimer v. General Cable Corp. (1956) 143 Cal.App.2d 293, 298-99.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)
